mechanism_narrative: A three-month rising trend in the Industrial Production Index signals expanding factory output and improving capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with positive three-month INDPRO momentum produce negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns more than 50 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above nine percent signals deep economic slack that guarantees prolonged zero-interest-rate policy and eventual fiscal stimulus, reducing equity discount rates and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirm a self-sustaining consumer demand loop where income growth fuels spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Months where both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase show negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 45 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds eight percent, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal a bottoming in the sector most responsible for the downturn, triggering a re-rating of financial and construction equities that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent, positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns more than 50 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month changes in the consumer price index below 0.1 index points signal disinflationary pressure that prompts market expectations of additional Federal Reserve asset purchases, causing a rapid risk-on repricing and positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL prints below 0.1 month-over-month are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production surprises markets that had priced in a V-shaped recovery, triggering immediate portfolio de-risking and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Months with declining INDPRO show positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns more than 55 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a federal funds rate below twenty-five basis points and a month-over-month decline in the consumer price index creates a sharply negative real rate environment that forces capital out of money markets and into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
regime_hint: deflation scare under zero lower bound
failure_condition: Instances where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent and CPIAUCSL declines month-over-month produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 45 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter change in real GDP signals the end of recessionary contraction and the start of an earnings recovery, leading to a sustained reallocation into equities and positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth coincides with negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations.
